core_mechanism: a Banach fixed-point / contraction-mapping well-posedness argument for a semilinear evolution equation driven by noise, carried out in time-weighted fractional-order norms so that only local Lipschitz continuity is required, yielding a unique maximal local solution that is continued until blow-up
brief_reason: The abstract establishes existence, uniqueness, and pathwise regularity of maximal local solutions for semilinear stochastic evolution equations with merely locally Lipschitz coefficients by formulating the Lipschitz condition in a specific time-weighted norm, which is the hallmark of a contraction/fixed-point local-well-posedness scheme accommodating rough initial data.
